name: Fillet of Catfish Bayou Lafourche
recipeInstructions:
1. 4 Catfish fillets (5-7 oz each)
2. 1/2 cup clarified butter
3. 1 cup all-purpose flour
4. Salt to taste
5. Cayenne pepper to taste
6. 1/2 cup champagne or dry white wine
7. 12 fresh oysters, shucked
8. 2 lemons, juice of
9. 2 tablespoons shallots, chopped
10. 2 tablespoons tarragon, chopped fresh
11. 1/2 teaspoon tarragon, dried
12. 2 tablespoons green onion, chopped
13. 1/2 cup unsalted butter
14. 2 tablespoons chives, chopped
1. Thaw frozen fish according to package directions.
2. Season catfish with salt and cayenne; dust with flour, shaking off excess.
3. Heat clarified butter in a large heavy skillet over medium heat.
4. Place fillets in the skillet, flat side up; sauté until brown on one side.
5. Turn fillets and continue to sauté until brown on both sides, then remove to heated plates.
6. Deglaze the skillet with champagne or wine; add oysters, oyster liquor, lemon juice, shallots, fresh or dried tarragon, and green onions.
7. Cook until oysters begin to curl, then remove and place 3 on each fillet.
8. Reduce liquid in the skillet until a glaze forms, then add cold butter, a few chips at a time, swirling the pan constantly (do not stir, as spots will develop and butter solids and liquids will separate).
9. Continue adding butter; it will emulsify, creating a smooth creamy sauce.
10. Add chives, adjust seasoning with salt and cayenne, and pour over oysters.
